title: IBM RS/6000 Database Marketing Training Engagement (Aberdeen Group, June 1995)
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
abstract: This letter confirms Aberdeen Group's engagement to develop and present a four-hour database marketing training module for IBM's RS/6000 Division, covering the business drivers for distributed database servers, database-hardware platform selection criteria, the RS/6000's strengths against Sun, HP, AT&T, and DEC, and detailed competitive positioning of the five major relational databases (DB2/6000, CA-Ingres, Informix, Oracle, and Sybase) available on the RS/6000 platform. The module is designed to sharpen the marketing knowledge of IBM's RS/6000 marketing specialists and Business Partners for customer-facing database sales engagements.
importance_rationale: Documents Aberdeen's role as a third-party educator within major IT vendor training programs, and provides a detailed snapshot of the commercial relational database competitive landscape in mid-1995, when the RDBMS market was consolidating around Oracle, Sybase, and Informix.
relevance_rationale: The specific RS/6000 platform and 1995 RDBMS competitive dynamics are historical; the document has limited ongoing applicability beyond its archival record of mid-1990s database market positioning.
prescience_rationale: This is a training engagement confirmation letter and curriculum outline rather than a market prediction or analytical report; prescience assessment is not applicable.
